title: User cannot access a shared folder

ticket.description: The user receives Access Denied when opening the approved Projects shared folder.

actions.label: Ask whether other network resources work
actions.result: Other network resources work; only the Projects share is denied.

evidence.title: Scope
evidence.body: The issue is isolated to one shared folder, not general network access.

actions.label: Verify the user's identity and business need
actions.result: Identity and department assignment are verified.

actions.label: Check the approved share access group
actions.result: The user is missing the Projects share security group.

evidence.title: Root Cause
evidence.body: The user lacks the group membership used to grant access.

actions.label: Add the user to the approved least-privilege group
actions.result: The approved group membership is added.

actions.label: Have the user sign out and back in, then test access
actions.result: The user can open the share and create a test file.

evidence.title: Verification
evidence.body: Share access works after the approved group change.

actions.label: Document identity check, permissions fix, and verification
actions.result: Ticket is documented.

actions.label: Grant Everyone Full Control
actions.result: This exposes the share to unauthorized users.

actions.label: Close the ticket without verifying access
actions.result: The issue is not proven resolved.

grading.summary: A strong permissions workflow scopes the issue, verifies identity and need, fixes least-privilege group membership, verifies access, and documents the ticket.

ticket.description: The user accidentally deleted a contract document from a shared folder and needs it restored.

actions.label: Ask for file name, location, and last known good time
actions.result: The user provides the file name, folder, and last known good time.

evidence.title: Restore Scope
evidence.body: The affected file and restore point are known.

actions.label: Check the approved backup or previous-version source
actions.result: A successful backup from last night contains the file.

actions.label: Restore only the deleted file
actions.result: The file is restored without overwriting unrelated data.

evidence.title: Restore Complete
evidence.body: Only the requested file was restored.

actions.label: Have the user open the restored file
actions.result: The user confirms the restored file opens and has the expected content.

actions.label: Document restore source, file restored, and verification
actions.result: Ticket is documented.

actions.label: Restore the entire shared folder
actions.result: This may overwrite newer files that were not affected.

penalty.reason: A whole-share restore is too broad for one deleted file.

actions.label: Close after starting the restore
actions.result: The restore is not confirmed.

penalty.reason: Restores must be verified before closure.

grading.summary: A good restore workflow scopes the file, finds a valid backup, restores only what is needed, verifies with the user, and documents the result.

title: Remote user cannot connect to VPN

ticket.description: A remote user cannot connect to the corporate VPN before a customer meeting.

actions.label: Ask for the VPN error and whether internet works
actions.result: Web browsing works. The VPN fails during MFA approval.

evidence.title: VPN Error
evidence.body: The VPN reaches MFA, so basic internet access is not the root cause.

actions.label: Verify internet access outside VPN
actions.result: External websites load normally.

actions.label: Check account status and MFA registration
actions.result: The account is active, but MFA is still registered to the user's old phone.

evidence.title: Root Cause
evidence.body: MFA prompts are going to the wrong registered device.

actions.label: Reset MFA using the approved identity process
actions.result: MFA is re-registered on the user's current phone after identity verification.

actions.label: Verify VPN login with MFA
actions.result: The user approves MFA and connects successfully.

actions.label: Document MFA issue, correction, and VPN verification
actions.result: Ticket is documented.

actions.label: Disable MFA to get the user connected
actions.result: This weakens remote-access security.

actions.label: Reinstall the VPN client first
actions.result: The evidence points to MFA registration, not the VPN client.

grading.summary: A strong VPN workflow separates internet connectivity from VPN authentication, checks account and MFA state, fixes MFA securely, verifies connection, and documents the ticket.

ticket.description: The user received a phone call from someone claiming to be IT and asking for their password and MFA code.

actions.label: Tell the user not to share credentials or MFA codes
actions.result: The user ends the call and confirms no credentials will be shared.

actions.label: Collect caller number, time, and request details
actions.result: The caller impersonated IT and pressured the user for password and MFA approval.

evidence.title: Incident Details
evidence.body: The call matches a vishing attempt.

actions.label: Report the incident to security
actions.result: Security receives the details for investigation and awareness.

actions.label: Check for suspicious sign-ins or MFA prompts
actions.result: No suspicious sign-ins are found.

actions.label: Educate the user on vishing and MFA fatigue
actions.result: The user understands that IT will not ask for passwords or MFA codes.

actions.label: Document incident report, account check, and education
actions.result: Ticket is documented.

actions.label: Ask for the user's password to test the account
actions.result: This repeats the attacker's unsafe request.

penalty.reason: Technicians should never ask users to disclose passwords.

actions.label: Ignore the report because no password was shared
actions.result: Security incidents still need reporting.

penalty.reason: Reported social engineering attempts should be documented and escalated.

grading.summary: A strong social-engineering response stops credential sharing, collects details, escalates, checks for compromise, educates, and documents the incident.

ticket.description: Monthly Windows updates fail repeatedly with a storage-related error.

actions.label: Review Windows Update history and error details
actions.result: The update repeatedly fails with an insufficient free space error.

evidence.title: Update Error
evidence.body: The error points to low disk space.

actions.label: Check free space on the system drive
actions.result: The system drive has less than 2 GB free.

actions.label: Free space with Disk Cleanup or Storage Sense
actions.result: Temporary files and old update cleanup free more than 12 GB.

actions.label: Retry Windows Update
actions.result: The update installs successfully.

actions.label: Restart and verify update status
actions.result: After restart, Windows Update reports the system is up to date.

actions.label: Document error, cleanup, update install, and verification
actions.result: Ticket is documented.

actions.label: Disable Windows Update
actions.result: The workstation would miss security updates.

actions.label: Reimage the workstation first
actions.result: A reimage is unnecessary before fixing low disk space.

penalty.reason: Reimaging is too invasive for this issue.

grading.summary: A good Windows Update workflow reviews the error, checks disk space, frees space safely, retries the update, verifies after reboot, and documents the result.
